title: micropus californicus var. angustifolius
intro: Micropus californicus var. angustifolius, also known as California Yerba Buena, is a plant species that belongs to the Asteraceae family. It has been traditionally used in herbal medicine for its anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antiviral properties, which can be attributed to its high concentration of sesquiterpenes and other bioactive compounds. The medicinal properties of California Yerba Buena have been associated with several health benefits, including reducing fever, alleviating respiratory issues, and soothing skin irritations. In herbal preparations, the plant is typically used as a tea or tincture, with some practitioners also recommending its use in salves and topical creams.
